title: Assignments
text: Assignments In ProcScript, the equal sign ( = ) assigns the result of an expression to a variable, parameter, field, or status function. The general format of a ProcScript assignment is: Variable = Expression Field = Expression @ Field = Expression $ Function = Expression where: Variable —specifies the literal name of a local varaible, component variable or global variable Field —specifies the literal name of a field to which Expression is assigned @ Field —specifies the literal name of a field that holds the name of the field to which Expression is assigned $ Function —specifies the literal name of a status function to which Expression is assigned Expression —specifies the expression that is being used to generate a value. An expression can always be assigned to, or substituted in, a string. Uniface performs implicit data type conversion if the expression is of a data type other then String. Example: Assignments in ProcScript The following ProcScript example shows a basic assignment: myVar = "My expression" Copy The following ProcScript example shows an assignment in combination with a ProcScript function: $1 = $lowercase("My expression") Copy The following ProcScript example shows an assignment to an indirected field: FIELD1 = "" FIELDNAME = "FIELD1" @FIELDNAME = $lowercase("My expression") ; Result: FIELD1 contains "my expression" Copy Related Links = (compute) Assigns the value of an expression to a field, variable, or function.
